curriculum_alignment.subject: mathematics
curriculum_alignment.seeds_of_change: Generate and interpret the sine graph from the unit circle using radians, then model real periodic phenomena with y = a sin(bx) + k by identifying and explaining amplitude, period, and vertical shift.

verse_ality.driving_question: How do sine graphs grow from the unit circle, and how can we use them to model real waves that matter to life below water?

knowing_world.focus: Introduce radians as a natural angle measure, then trace the sine graph from the unit circle by following the y-coordinate of a rotating point.
key_concepts: Arc length and the unit circle
Sine as the y-coordinate on the unit circle
Maxima and minima on the sine curve
tools: Interactive: Unit Circle Activity - Tracing the Sine Graph (canvas_file_embed): https://canvas.instructure.com/courses/12822206/files/314020088/download
activities: Convert key angles between degrees and radians (π/2, π, 3π/2, 2π).
Use the unit circle sketch to observe how height changes and how that becomes the sine wave.
Respond to prompts: where is the maximum/minimum, and why does the shape match vertical movement?

human_story.focus: Connect sine waves to ocean tides, sound waves, and sonar used in underwater exploration and marine protection.
justice_prompts: Many communities rely on ocean tides for food and energy. How can understanding wave graphs support fairer access to resources?
What patterns might fish, divers, or whales experience below water?

expressive_self.focus: Use creative reflection to connect repeating rhythms in mathematics to rhythms in personal experience (music, emotion, energy, motion).
activities: Complete: “A sine wave reminds me of…”
Sketch your own repeating rhythm (visual, emotional, or musical).
Record a short reflection on how rhythm shapes your days or relationships.
Reflect: When have you experienced “waves” of energy, emotion, or motion?

designed_world.focus: Model real tidal behaviour by transforming a sine graph using y = a sin(bx) + k, matching height, frequency, and baseline to a simplified tide cycle.
design_challenges: Use the interactive model to adjust a, b, and k to fit a simplified tide pattern.
Explain how amplitude, period, and vertical shift connect to the real tide cycle.
Consider how modelling could support underwater mapping or marine energy design.
tools: Interactive: Tidal Modeling with Sine Transformations (canvas_file_embed): https://canvas.instructure.com/courses/12822206/files/314020083/download

regulation_supports: Move: Rock gently side to side like a sine wave. Inhale as you rise, exhale as you dip.
Reflect: Notice how predictable patterns calm or energise you.
wellbeing_prompts: How does your body respond to predictable patterns? When do waves calm or energise you?

pathways.name: Final Task: Mapping Ocean Waves (SDG 14 – Life Below Water)
pathways.focus: Use sine graphs and transformations to model a wave or sonar behaviour that supports sustainable marine research or energy generation.
products: A graph using y = a sin(bx) + k to model a wave, with labelled amplitude, period, and vertical shift.
Written explanations for how a, b, and k affect the model (and what each means in context).
A written or voice reflection linking the model to ocean sustainability (e.g., sonar mapping or tidal research).
Optional: creative output from the Expressive Self section.
criteria: Conceptual Understanding – Explain the function and transformations clearly.
Fluency & Accuracy – Correct graphing and correct use of parameters.
Application to Problems – Realistic application of the graph to an SDG context.
Independence & Reflection – Evidence of thoughtful design and reasoning.

required: Basic understanding of coordinates and reading graphs
Comfort with angle measure in degrees (and readiness to convert to radians)
Use of a calculator for π approximations where needed

builds_toward: Graphs of trig functions (cosine and tangent)
Modelling periodic phenomena and solving trig equations
